entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::354 - Tien meets Dade
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Tien and I finally met baby Dade today, and boy did she swoon.

entry.title: 33::188 - Tien at Tunnel View
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::365 - Collage of Home
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::364 - Dinner in LX
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::362 - Nhu
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::361 - Curls
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::360 - River night life
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::359 - Alley jump
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

The sun finally came out the day we decided to leave.

entry.title: 33::358 - Boats on Hoi An
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::357 - Bà Triệu, Hội An
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::356 - Nha Trang Streetlife 1
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::355 - Vinpearl Land Underwater World
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::354 - The second "island"
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Tien and I went on a tour today and this was the second island we visited. Tien rode an ostrich, we saw a bear ride a bicycle and a motorbike, and we went swimming in some the water even though it was a bit chilly. The problem though was that it wasn't an island at all, as you can see by the map.

entry.title: 33::353 - Harbor at Nha Trang
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::352 - Bunkbed bus
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

This wasn't quite what we had in mind when we bought our bus tickets.

entry.title: 33::351 - Up in Bitexco Tower
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::350 - Christmas in Saigon
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::349 Minh and Thu say goodbye
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

They've been married for 5 years, but America still won't let her into the country. He came back for her interview at the consulate hoping she'd come back to America with him, but with no specific reason (which is typical) they said they couldn't give her a visa yet, so once again she's left behind as he heads back to the USA, on Christmas Eve no less.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::346 - At the Chau Doc temple
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::347 - Tien in the garden
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::345 - Over Rach Gia
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

The boat ride out to Phu Quoc was so turbulent that many people on board were throwing up repeatedly for more than an hour. Tien didn't fare quite that bad, but also was seasick, so we decided to fly back to the mainland.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::343 - Seasons greetings from Bai Sao
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

We rode down to Bai Sao today and found it almost unrecognizable as the placid paradise we discovered a year and a half ago. The wind was strong, the waves were large, the ocean was upset, and the beach had been reshaped into a short, steep drop directly down to the water. Apparently we had hit it at the perfect season last time. Still, it was gorgeous and we had a good time.

It was my joke this year that our white Christmas would involve white sandy beaches, but it was true!

entry.title: 33::342 - Red lights on the river
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Tien and I arrived at Phu Quoc today. We checked into our old hotel, took a nap and went shopping. We ended up at the night market for dinner. This was taken where the night market street ends at the river.

entry.title: 33::341 - Sunset in Rach Gia
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

We missed the boat to Phu Quoc by 5 minutes, so we spent the night in Rach Gia.

This could be a scene from nearly any Vietnamese pop song. They're all sad and heartbroken.

entry.title: 33::340 - Cow out back
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::339 - Nhu
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::338 - Luan
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::337 - Minh and Thu
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::336 - Helmets
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Helmet style has only gotten better since the last time Tien and I were in VN 18 months ago.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::334 - Ngoc buồn cười
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::332 - At the night market
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::332 - Tien at Cafe A Chay
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::330 - Nhu and Tien
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::329 - Ngoc
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::327 - Tien in Beijing
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

We nearly missed our flight from Beijing to Saigon. It's 33ºF / 1ºC here, which we weren't really prepared for.

entry.title: 33::325 - Tien at SFO
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

This is the day we left America for 3 months, Tien's first time returning to Vietnam since her arrival nearly a year and a half ago.

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

On our last day in America we had packed up most of our cold weather stuff so we had to improvise on keeping warm when we went out to pick up the last few things we needed for our trip. My peacoat looks like a dress on Tien.

entry.title: 33::323 - The last ice we'll see this year
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Tien and I bought some apple cider and walked around, ending up at Union Square where there were people ice skating. She still has never gone ice skating.

entry.title: 33::322 - Repairing cable
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Now that I don't have a job I have a ton of free time and I'm amazed at how cool life is in my neighborhood. I see all sorts of things during the day that I never used to see. Today seemed like a week day too, I think it's the tourist off-season in SF due to Thanksgiving.

entry.title: 33::321 - The girls at Neja
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::319 - Seeing Wendy off
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::318 - Jonathan and Angel
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::317 - Family
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Tien and I sat on the couch waiting for my aunt and brother to pick us up, but soon retreated to play Wii.

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I got sick with the flu this evening. The girls decorated the tree, partly by repeatedly throwing ornaments into the branches until they stuck somewhere.

entry.title: 33::313 - Bounce Flash
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Fava and I were discussing bounce flash a lot, so I took this long exposure to show just what is illuminated when you bounce into a corner. That's a pretty big light diffuser if you ask me.

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

This morning Tien and I rode over to Ghirardelling Square to pick up some cupcakes from Kara's to take to Dan and Cass.

entry.title: 33::312 - Dan Fava, November 2011
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::310 - Dessert for my last day of work
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Tien's last day was yesterday, and mine was today, so we went out for sushi and Sophie's Crepes in Japantown, did some shopping, and enjoyed the beginning of ~4 months of freedom and traveling.

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Cameron, Gaspare and I stayed up well into the morning solving the world's problems.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::301 - Waiting for Kat and Kyung
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Like an idiot I forgot to photograph Kat and Kyung after they arrived, but they did arrive and we did watch Totem and it was fantastic.

entry.title: 33::300 - Light holes in metal
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I was reading this article on Apple's supply chain and got sorely distracted once I reached the part about the lasers cutting holes through metal. I decided to take a photo the result.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I spent over two and a half hours at the DMV renewing my driver's license today. Now I can go to the Botanical Gardens for free... just like old times when it was free.

entry.title: 33::298 - In that bloodshot eye
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I've been playing with the Rokinon 85mm on two sets of Fotodiox tubes. The Rokinon is nice for its manual aperture, but it's not very clear at high apertures, which is less than suitable for macro. Still, it's not a bad setup. I wasn't totally happy with this shot, but for a 365 it'll do.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::295 - Late lunch for Raymond
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::294 - My beautiful Mrs.
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

My 365 shots don't always come out as artistic or wonderful as I'd like them to, but they do serve to remind me what I was doing in my past, for instance, two years ago today I had a stranger sleeping on my shoulder while riding on a bus in Vietnam.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::290 - GoPro Hero 2
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

HD2, Hero2, I'm not sure what this camera is called but I picked one up. I haven't done a solid comparison to the previous model yet but there are some small improvements that were easy to find.

entry.title: 33::289 - Obama's entourage
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Obama's motorcade rolled into my neighborhood today shortly after the police towed every vehicle whose driver was stupid enough to miss seeing the no parking signs, including my scooter. We weren't allowed to go out the front door or open the windows of our building. I had to go out the back and walk to the impound lot, since the bus route had been cut off as well, and pay $450 to get my scooter back. Maybe I should start riding a bicycle.

Video on my youtube page.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::285 - Command Key
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::284 - Cirque du Soleil set up shop in SF today!
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I got an e-mail from Cirque today saying they had arrived in SF and were setting up shop, so after doing a job in a nearby neighborhood I cruised over on my scooter to snap a photo of their signature tent, Le Grand Chapiteau.

entry.title: 33::283 - Bedtime
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::282 - Market Street
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Whenever I take a shot like this I lament the fact that San Francisco has very few publicly accessible places that are high above ground.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::277 - Old Tech
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::276 - Occupy Parking Spots
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

A secondary protest to the #OccupySF protest happened this morning in front of the Wells Fargo HQ on Montgomery. Not sure what this was specifically about but they were obviously protesting big banks. They said they were not part of #OccupySF, but agreed with it.

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

A girl with an iPhone cued me into this fantastic phenomenon on my way home, so I hurried the remaining two blocks, grabbed my trusty new 75-300 and headed out on my scooter to find the best vantage point.

entry.title: 33::274 - Your butt break my skeleton
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::273 - Italian Pride at the Columbus Day Parade
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::272 - Dragon People
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I passed a TON of these dragon people in the stockton tunnel today.

entry.title: 33::271 - Steve Jobs' memorial
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

This memorial to Steve Jobs outside the downtown SF Apple store wasn't there the evening of his death, at least not when I stopped by, but in the following days it grew and grew.

entry.title: 33::270 - Fresh and clean
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::269 - Vespa
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::266 - From a hill in SF
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Karen and I went geocaching today. Incidentally, Oct 3 was 10 years after I found my first cache.

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I didn't take a photo today, so here's a screengrab from a video I took with my GoPro mounted on my scooter helmet.

entry.title: 33::264 - Star Bright
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::237 - Lambo
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I found this in a back alley on my way to shop at a thrift store. Quite polarized. Speaking of which, I wish I would've had a polarizer to work those reflections, but hey, all I had was a phone. Incidentally, this is not the first Lamborghini I've run into randomly on the streets of SF.

entry.title: 33::261 - Bon voyage, parents
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::260 - Under a tree
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Tien took two days off while my parents were here and we actually had time to go enjoy our life! Awesome!

entry.title: 33::259 - Mom and Dad
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

My parents came to visit!

entry.title: 33::258 - Tien at Tomo

entry.title: 33::255 - South on New Montgomery

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

This is the view of my office from Montgomery Station.

entry.title: 33::250 - Bri and Lily, September 2011
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::247 - Jörgen Danielsson, September 2011
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Tonight I was invited out by Scott to have dinner with Jörgen, a friend of his from Sweden who I'd met a few years ago on his last trip to America, and have kept in touch with online. We went to a fantastic restaurant called The Daily Grill.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::246 - Non protest traffic snarl
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

No, that wasn't #opbart that was blocking traffic on Market street tonight.

entry.title: 33::245 - Train Tracks and Time Travel
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Technically, whenever you travel you're traveling through time.

entry.title: 33::244 - Wedding photo-fu by the forest
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Dan Fava shot Ariana and Matt's wedding today.

entry.title: 33::241 - Dan Fava, September, 2011
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::240 - Yo
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::238 - Vince in Chinatown
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::237 - Making up
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::236 - Wild Chinese Animals
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

On my way home from work I ran into a pack of these wild animals terrorizing chinatown, making a racket and chasing some nice looking folks, very official looking, up Stockton Street.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::234 - G city lights
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::233 - Asus E35M1-I
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

The motherboard for my new FreeNAS box.

entry.title: 33::232 - The Expected
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

One of the Anonymous slogans is "Expect us." The cops and the BART riders expected Anonymous tonight, so much so that most commuters went home early leaving mostly protestors and cops parading up and down Market street. I walked with them for a while to take photos, video, and stand up for freedom of speech.

There's something awesome about a man in a pin striped suit riding a bicycle, even without a mask and a megaphone.

Videos of the protest are on my youtube

entry.title: 33::231 - Hairstyling
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Tien has been working on finding a style for Ariana's wedding, even up until the last minute before I ride her to work on the scooter.

entry.title: 33::230 - Late Summer Evening Fog
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Lately we've been getting a dense fog setting in around sunset here in downtown SF. It makes for some great atmosphere.

entry.title: 33::229 - German Giunta, August 2011
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::228 - Flat rate parking
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I doubt having this car in their private lot is good for business.

entry.title: 33::227 - Morning with The Icon
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

There were unusual cloud patterns in the sky today. Unusual for San Francisco anyway.

entry.title: 33::226 - Mason to The Rock
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::224 - Rob Spectre, August 2011
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::223 - Noel and Laura
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::222 - Certified pre-owned pets
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::221 - Down Jessie
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::220 - 15 minutes in SOMA
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

180 photos over 15 minutes.

entry.title: 33::219 - Under the fog blanket
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::218 - muBARTek
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Towards the end of work I heard sirens and helicopters, and knew that Anonymous had indeed come to town. Ah, the good old days of masked men on market street swinging around light poles, skipping along and telling everybody to not be afraid because they are from the internet. Except this time it wasn't like that. It was interested crowds, shouting protestors, stoic riot cops, and a lot of irritated commuters.

Video footage here: www.youtube.com/watch?v=zU8T-zxNGSA

entry.title: 33::217 - Living Space
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::215 - Secrets between heads
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::216 - New camera
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

This is my new camera toy. I've finally gotten a grasp on how best to use it to take the videos I want, but also found some neat still photo tricks to do with it as well. If you want to see my videos, don't look here! Video doesn't belong on Flickr, imho. Head on over to my Youtube page for my videos.

This photo was taken with the 85mm Rokinon on a short extension tube for macro focus, a setup that's working out really well for me.

entry.title: 33::213 - Graduation party
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Tien's school had a little graduation party for the folks who have recently passed the cosmetology certification test.

entry.title: 33::214 - ereading
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Tien frequently reads on her iPhone, and I just got a Nook. Tonight was an ereading night. I'm reading Alone Together.

entry.title: 33::212 - Tien and I in August 2011
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

The T3i and Sigma 10-20mm are a perfect self portrait combo.

entry.title: 33::211 - Telecom Fashion
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I passed this boutique display near Union Square on my way to work this morning. In-f'in-credible dress made out of telecom wires. I'll just leave it at that and forgo the nerdcore wet dreams.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::209 - Journey from SF
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Gerald had been staying with Tien and I for a week. His journey home began on the sidewalk outside my house in this red Mini Cooper.

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Gerald and I walked a few blocks over to Telegraph Hill and laid on Kearny Street for a while talking, drinking and watching the fog roll in through the skyscrapers. We then got Thai food for dinner, then at 10pm we went out to 1015 Folsom and didn't get home until after 3am.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::105 Tien at King Philip's Shipwreck
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Tien and I went down to Ocean Beach by my old house to check out the remains of the King Philip shipwreck from 1878, which was covered up by sand for the entire time I lived about a block away. Here you can see the bow and the stern which shows how large of a ship this was.

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I realized recently that I forgot to post some of the photos from my 365. This is one of them.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::204 - Gerald, Nate and Devaki
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::203 - Yerba Buena and the eastern Bay Bridge, July 2011
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::202 - Gerald Kahre, July 2011
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::201 - Devaki and Nathan
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

The replacement Mural... somehow it's pretty underwhelming.

entry.title: 33::198 - D300 Finale
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

This is the last photo I took with my D300 before selling it to this girl. I am now officially a Canon man.

entry.title: 33::197 - Sara Stearns, July 2011
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Sara came over for dinner and photo talk tonight. It was good to catch up with her before her long trip to Poland and Spain.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::195 - Stretching ballerina
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

This is a new art piece in the front window of a studio on my street.

entry.title: 33::194 - Falling up through reflections again
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::193 - Flat recycled trash
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I thought it was remarkable to see a flat screen television thrown out with the trash.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::189 - Wooden interior Fiat
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::190 - Foldcycle
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::188 - Passport or Cub
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I could've sworn the Honda Passport was a four wheeled vehicle... Oh yeah, it is. WTF? Talk about unoriginal marketing. Why not use one of the other over 250,000 english words for the newer vehicle, which is the SUV, or just keep the original name, the Honda Super Cub?

entry.title: 33::187 - Blooter
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::184 - Chinatown Alley
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::181 - Attention to detail
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::180 - Peeking
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::179 - Happy Birthday Raymond
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I think this Rokinon lens might look better on the D300 than it did on the D3s. Definitely going to have to pick it up for my Canon.

entry.title: 33::176 - Installing Lion, drinking beer
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

On my way to work I spotted this crazy handlebar configuration, passed it, then walked back to photograph it.

Lately I've been using the T3i's liveview in manual focus mode with the Sigma 30mm ƒ/1.4 and have been very happy with the results. You have to move the focus square to where you want it in the composed photo, then tap the zoom-in view button twice to get a full-scale focus view. This lets you nail your focus and your composition simultaneously. This technique works on other cameras as well, and in fact was exactly how I used the D300 and D3s with the PC-E 24mm in tilt/shift mode to get proper TS focus.

entry.title: 33::174 - Explosions in the sky
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

It was really cold and windy at first. Tien and I went up to the top of Russian hill, a few blocks from our house, and and stood there for 30 minutes as the crowd amassed. By the time the fireworks began shortly after 9:30 it felt much warmer. It was the first time either Tien or I had seen a proper firework show in SF and it was really beautiful.

entry.title: 33::173 - Invisible Sushi
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Tien was imitating the skepticism of her first time eating sushi a year ago.

entry.title: 33::172 - Tien at Fort McDowell
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Tien and I went hiking on Angel Island today. I always love it there, and I always enjoy taking photos at Fort McDowell. I found this beautiful light coming in from outside and decided to compliment it with a beautiful girl.

This was my first time using the Eye-Fi outside of the city, and boy does it suck out there. No geotagging anywhere that you need it.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::166 - Ducati
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

These bikes are really popular in SF, probably because they don't have fairings to steal.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::168 - Rain
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

We hadn't had much rain for a while until today. It wasn't cold though, so that was great.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::165 - Exploring Russian Hill
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Once in a while Tien and I go off for a walk in the evening to explore the stairwells and alleys around our neighborhood. This alley was up a wooden stairwell that I initially thought was private property. I asked three kids with scooters and helmets that were coming down if it was their house and they said no, just an alley that was really pretty, then took off down some killer steep hill on their scooters. This is what Tien and I found in the alley.

entry.title: 33::164 - Good Morning Frank Chu
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I ran into Frank Chu on the way to work this morning, I'd never seen him out so early before. I stopped and talked with him while taking his photo and he gave me the usual story. He's going to the UN in 2013 to unveil the evil of the 12 galaxies, impeach Bill Clinton and get paid for being a movie star.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Wandering alleys on my way to work always makes it easy to hit my daily 365 project goals. Found this several blocks from my house this morning.

entry.title: 33::160 - The Sokols
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Jake and Rachel came out to SFBA and spent the night up here in SF. We went downtown for sushi and then off to drinks before wandering back through Union Square and Chinatown around midnight.

entry.title: 33::159 - Shopping
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Tien and I went shopping for a gift for her friend Thi who passed her cosmetology certification test recently.

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Tien and I went down to the new office to draw some power and networking maps before everybody filled the space up. There were some nice bare walls and diffused northern light coming in, so I took a few snaps.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::156 - Ali Mousavi, June 2011
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I found the Sigma 30mm ƒ/1.4 long after I had my Nikkor 35mm ƒ/2, so I could never justify buying this fantastic lens. Switching to Canon was a perfect reason to pick it up. I was showing it to my friend and co-worker Ali and took his photo.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::154 - Image Number 0
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

The first shot of the Canon T3i was actually numbered 0. Self portrait because, hey, it's got a flip-out screen so why not?

So far, I think that for me, switching off the D3s to a newer, cheaper camera was a good change. I do not think that the D3s is $4500 better than this camera. One thing I'm enjoying already is the 50% more megapixels this camera offers. One thing I'm already missing is the killer tone curve and details in the dark areas the D3s had. Aside from that, experiencing photography from a consumer standpoint on a new platform is refreshing. The feature set is different, the interface is different.

One other thing I love about the T3i so far is that if I leave the LCD closed, it's just like using a film camera. All the important info is in the viewfinder.

Now, I need to accessorize with some great glass, because that's the most important part of a camera system these days.

entry.title: 33::153 - Motorbike and sidecar
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::152 - The new intersection
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::151 - Got a job
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Tien decided to work at Lee's Sandwiches with a friend to see what it's like working in America and make a little extra money.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::149 - Found black couch
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::148 - Kyung Park
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Went out to lunch with Kyung today and he brought along his Sony NEX-5 with a Voigtlander 35mm f/1.4 on it. 35mm is such a great perspective for a 1.5x crop.

entry.title: 33::147 - Sky Writing Nonsense
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Serious advertising fail, I have no idea what the message here is. It was a nice stunt though. 5 planes acting as a dot matrix label maker in the sky.

entry.title: 33::146 - Hiding fruit in the basement
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Many of the shops in SF have doors like this near the street where boxes can be delivered straight to the basement via ramp. It's a pretty efficient way to get deliveries inside and out of the way, but I always manage to catch it at the wrong time.

entry.title: 33::145 - Skyping with mom
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::144 - Laying new track
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Part of the construction on our street this week involved replacing the Jackson and Mason cable car rail intersection. I snapped this with my iPhone on my way to get coffee when they were putting it in.

entry.title: 33::143 - Repaired Street
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

This, apparently, is their idea of fixing the bridge. That dark horizontal line where the pillow carrying person is walking is not a gentle drop on a motor scooter. =/

entry.title: 33::142 - Construction on Broadway
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::141 - Wet Streets
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

June 1st and it's pouring rain.

entry.title: 33::140 - Empty Street
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

This was the first day of repairs on the Broadway Tunnel Bridge, which is the area where the van is driving, directly over the Broadway Tunnel. I've never seen the street so devoid of cars.

entry.title: 33::139 - Photographing Drake's Bay
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Tien and I went with Igor and Helen up to Point Reyes for a picnic with a bunch of their friends. Igor let me take his Olympus E-PL2 out for a spin and it was a joy to use, but I'm still on the fence about the images and movies it made.

entry.title: 33::138 - Flowers for Tien
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Tien loves sunflowers, but has never seen them grow naturally. We're still trying to find a place to view them growing in the ground.

entry.title: 33::137 - Carnivale
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Tien, Karen and I went down to Carnivale today. It was a casual street fair with a mellow, good vibe. Not too crowded, the food was great, and the weather was perfect.

entry.title: 33::136 - Bowling
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Tonight I went bowling with Tien and a bunch of her friends in Daly City. There's something weird about bowling culture... similar to roller skating culture, I guess.

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I think I may begin playing more with cheap hot lights.

entry.title: 33::134 - Just walk on the street
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

The sidewalks in Chinatown are so packed with produce and shoppers in the morning that walking in the street is very common. I've heard tourists balk at it, but there's really no other way if you actually want to get somewhere. This is the usual 9am scene at the bus stop nearest my house.

entry.title: 33::133 - Camera keeper
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I realized how I lost my LX3 the other day when I nearly lost my S95 in the same way: laying down with my camera in my hoodie pocket. At least that's my assumption. At any rate, it happened and how I keep a carabiner handy so I can secure my cam to my clothes.

entry.title: 33::132 - Still going up
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

It's been interesting to watch the construction of a new building within the brick walls of this older building. I'm rather looking forward to seen the end product.

entry.title: 33::131 - Practicing for the M1 license
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

A slightly bigger scooter and months without riding meant it was a good idea for Tien to practice a little before taking the M1 test, even though she's been riding for over 10 years. Unfortunately the SF DMV closes off its parking lot in off-hours, so we couldn't just go over there and practice on the actual testing ground.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::130 - Best Apocalypse Ever
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Tien and I had a picnic on Mt. Tamalpais. The weather was warm, the scenery was great, the food we brought was delicious, the drive was fun, and best of all, the world did not end.

entry.title: 33::127 - Mason & Broadway streetcar
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

This is a daily scene outside my house.

entry.title: 33::128 - Blake and Ramon
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Blake flew in yesterday with his friend Ramon to stay in SF for a few days. We met up for coffee this morning, over a year since last time I'd seen Blake in SF.

entry.title: 33::125 - Wiimote Nunchuck plug
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Tien and I have been playing Wii lately on my iMac using Dolphin. It's nice because the resolution is higher than if you were to use an actual Wii, but it's not as nice because there are software glitches and sometimes it runs slow.

entry.title: 33::126 - Nate Bennett, May, 2011
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Today I used a co-workers D7000 to take photos of all the people at my company.

entry.title: 33::124 - Yoyo Urbanite
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Tien and i went to the Triple Crown Yoyo competition in Washington Square Park today. There were so many amazing yoyo players there. This guy didn't compete but he looked pretty badass so I took his photo. I love his accessories all on display.

entry.title: 33::123 - I love this silly girl
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::122 - Bike to work day
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Over 20% of our in-office employees biked to work today! That means 2 of us.

entry.title: 33::121 - Atheist Advertisment
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I've seen countless billboards for Jesus and even a few for Islam, but until today I'd never seen one for atheism.

entry.title: 33::120 - SF Capitol building
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::118 - Top of Broadway
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I was walking home from Cole's Hardware and came across this familiar view. The weather has been sunny, but windy so far in May, which is such a tease.

entry.title: 33::119 - Memories and energy
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

My laptop hard disk died over the weekend, but thankfully I keep time machine backups and a bootable USB stick for re-installing and troubleshooting. I found all of these while digging in my backpack.

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I took the eye-fi out on a stroll today, and while its geotagging is decent, the fact that the only way you can import is over its incredibly slow wi-fi interface makes the device much less appealing.

entry.title: 33::116 - New toys
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

No, the iPhone isn't new, but the S95 that took this photo is, and so is the Eye-Fi card. Before going out and buying an Eye-FI "Pro" card, keep in mind that even the Eye-Fi "Pro" card cannot geotag RAW files, so you end up having to shoot RAW+JPG then run exiftool to migrate the exif data from the JPG to the RAW. The import tool is written in AIR though, so I have no idea why they don't give you the option to store RAW as DNG and include the GPS data there.

Also, so far my experience with this device reminds me of why I love Apple and buy their products. Speaking of Apple, the ability to transfer files to my iPhone so far does not work. Eye-Fi has been incredibly disappointing so far because it won't Geotag the file format I want, and it won't transfer to my phone, which are the two reasons I bought this thing.

entry.title: 33::115 - Here again
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::113 - Stopped bus #1
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

iPhone pressed against bus window at the bus stop at the south end of the Stockton Tunnel.

entry.title: 33::114 - Stopped bus #2
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

iPhone pressed against the bus window heading north on Stockton on my way home.

entry.title: 33::112 - News of the day, Bin Laden is dead
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::111 - How Weird 2011
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Tien and I met up with Karen, Mike and some of their friends at How Weird 2011 and had a great time. The weather was absolutely perfect. We got home at dusk and just as I was about to fall asleep I heard the television in the next room announcing Osama Bin Laden's death.

entry.title: 33::110 - Tien at Davenport
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Tien and I grabbed a zipcar and drove down to Monterey to check out the aquarium and the Secret Lives of Seahorses exhibit and took the coastal road both ways.

entry.title: 33::109 - Painted nails
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Tien paints her own nails.

entry.title: 33::108 - Infinity Rain
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I was walking by The Infinity Towers today and water was falling off of one of the balconies and coming down like rain onto the sidewalk.

entry.title: 33::107 - So long pro gear
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I sold all of this gear today.

entry.title: 33::102 - Leopard print street fighter
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::105 - Rainy April Morning
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Today I decided to sell all of my photography gear and start over from scratch, probably with Canon.

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I'm selling all of my photo gear in case anybody is interested: sfbay.craigslist.org/sfc/pho/2346084793.html
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::100 - Yellow Robot
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::99 - Smoking on Claude
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::98 - From 111 Sutter
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::97- Cherry Blossom Festival banner for Japan
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

At the Cherry Blossom Festival there was a Japanese man with two big banners that people could sign. He was then going to deliver them to the people recovering from the recent earthquakes in northern Japan.

entry.title: 33::85 - Bin Xu, April 2011
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I took the day off from my day job to do some corporate portraits for Magnet.com.

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

There is an argument going on in San Francisco about whether phonebooks should be delivered by default or or requested. From what I see, nobody wants them.

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Opening day at AT&T park

entry.title: 33::89 - Tien at Point Montara Lighthouse
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::94 - View from 139 Townsend, 5th floor
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::95 - Inspecting the green card
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Tien got her green card today and we went out to Hana Zen to celebrate. It's a remarkable ID card, the most intricate I've ever seen. Here you can see the microscopic holograph imagery reflecting light back onto Tien's face.

entry.title: 33::86 - Self portrait, April 2011
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::84 - Just paint around it
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I came out and found that somebody had painted around my scooter.

entry.title: 33::83 - Tien and Yoda
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::82 - Falling cherry blossoms
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

We were standing next to this tree when a sound like a huge plastic bag being blown very hard came overhead, but nothing was there. It happened again, and after looking around to see wtf was going on, cherry blossoms started falling out of the tree on a breeze all around us.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::81 - Graphed stars
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::80 - Air kisser
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::79 - Path from Presidio
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::78 - Tea with Bri
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I had tea with Brianna in her living room which has a garage door looking out onto Brannan street.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::72 - Office view
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

This is the view from my office. Remarkably, it was sunny this day. Lately it's been rainy almost all the time.

I decided to take more photos with my iPhone simply to document when and where I was.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::70 - Hospital view
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

After reading every poster on the wall in the doctor's office, I decided to take a photo of the decent view. I'd been sick with a throat and sinus infection for nearly three weeks; enough was enough.

entry.title: 33::68 - Rainy night at The View
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Our roomie Shanika was moving out after her two month internship was up, so Tien and I took her out to The View.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::67 - Surveying the collision
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Police survey a scene where this morning a crane hit a pedestrian who later died.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

The more I use the 105mm ƒ/2 DC, the more I love it. Being able to shoot at higher apertures while maintaining smooth bokeh is very nice.

Bare SB900 on-cam M 1/4

entry.title: 33::61 - Coming down Mason
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::60 - No Tsunami Yet
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

This photo was taken a few minutes after the tsunami waves were predicted to arrive in San Francisco. At the time, it seemed like everything was normal, though they did say that it would take several hours for all of the effects to pass.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::51 - Kevin Bayne, March 2011
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I ran into Kevin as he was getting off Caltrain, so we got coffee at The Creamery and caught up while walking to his office, which ended up being only two blocks from mine.

entry.title: 33::49 - Towed scooter
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I only took one photo today. Damn the mis-focus.

entry.title: 33::45 - Apple at YBCA
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Apple is preparing YBCA for their iPad event that's going on next week.

entry.title: 33::43 - The Lobby of Om
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Went over to meet a man at Founder's Den, which is in the same building as Om Records.

entry.title: 33::44 - Chinatown Mural, Pacific St
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

On my way to work today I found this new mural on the side of a dim sum restaurant on my street.

entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::37 - Wet and colorful morning
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::34 - Tien in the closet
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Tien and I moved to a different room in our house today after one of our room mates moved out. The closet shares its back wall with the furnace, so it's always warm, which is why Tien is standing in it.

entry.title: 33::33 - Carnival Splendor
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Tien and I were cruising up Embarcadero on the scooter enjoying the summery weather when we spotted this huge ship towering over the surrounding piers. The size of this vessel was quite impressive, I've never seen one as large.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::26 - Telegraph Hill
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Today was beautiful, so Tien and I took the scooter out and rode all over San Francisco. I mostly let her take photos while acting as her driver, but took a few photos for myself. The flags here caught my eye.

Also notable is how sharp the Sigma 85mm ƒ/1.4 is at ƒ/4.5.

entry.title: 33::27 - Alex Poston, February 2011
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::25 - Washing away tet
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Tet was yesterday, and there have been a lot of local celebrations including a lot of firecrackers which have left a big mess of red paper on the street. Regardless of what this guy is actually washing away, one thing is certain... Chinatown always needs to be washed.

entry.title: 33::24 - Stranger 1
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I had thought about doing a 365 project of stranger portraits, but the task was too daunting. There are so many interesting people in SF though so I decided to start taking them, starting with this girl who was in line at my regular lunch spot, Caffe Centro.

1/100 - www.100Strangers.com
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Been shooting with the Sigma 85mm ƒ/1.4 and sadly the $250 Rokinon 85mm ƒ/1.4 is better. If only it had auto-focus!

entry.title: 33::19 - Discards
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Found in an alley in Chinatown.

entry.title: 33::18 - Rob Spectre, January 2011
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

My good friend Rob was in town from NYC for work. Tien and I met up with him and Zach for dinner. I also got a new lens today and I saved my first shot for Rob. So here it is, my first photo from the Sigma 85mm ƒ/1.4. So far so good!
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::14 - Saints Peter and Paul Church construction
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

It's really feeling like spring in San Francisco, and the weather today was the best it's been in months. Tien and I went out to Cafe Greco for breakfast and then strolled through the art show in Washington Square where we found the Saints Peter and Paul Church undergoing some kind of construction.

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

First photo posted from a Nikon 105mm DC lens that I picked up off craiglist in a cash and lens swap for my 24mm PC-E.

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Today was a really busy day, I snapped this outside my house as I was getting home from work.

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I brought out one of my old favorite lenses that I never use anymore, the Sigma 10-20mm. It's a cropped sensor lens, so it's funky on the D3s, but even before I got a full frame camera I had backed off of the wide-angle perspective quite a bit. I've taken a lot of photos with this lens, many that I'm proud of, but I just lost interest in that perspective. I'm thinking about selling it, but not before I take it out to give it one last go.

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Found on Claude Lane in San Francisco along with some other beautiful murals.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::4 - Star Dummy
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Alternate title: Supermannequin

Today I quit over 150 Flickr groups, including almost everything that had an exclamation point in its name. I think I'll take another approach to interacting with Flickr, more focused.

entry.title: 33::3 - Sutter and Powell
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I'm still pretty impressed with how sharp this $250 lens is at ƒ/1.4.

entry.title: 33::2 - Kaiwen
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 33::1 - Thirty three, day one
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

First day of the new 365 set... updated my title correcting software and posted my first photo. I think it's off to a good start.
